intro: Most AI news is noise. Here's the short list that actually matters right now for anyone building or buying AI — with our plain read on what each one means for you. We link every source, so you can check us.

featured.headline: U.S. policy statement targets 'suppression of accuracy' in AI systems
featured.take: Read past the dry title: regulators are now putting AI accuracy on the record. The direction is clear — 'it seems to work' won't cut it for much longer. If you can't show a real number for how well your AI performs, that's becoming a liability, not just a nice-to-have. It's also exactly the thing we build for.

featured.headline: A Roomba recorded a woman on the toilet — the images ended up on Facebook
featured.take: The images leaked because the vacuum's data was shipped off to someone else's servers to be labeled. This is the whole reason we build AI you own and run on your own accounts. If your customers' data is the fuel, whose server it sits on is not a small detail.

featured.headline: Prof ordered an in-person final over AI-cheating fears — scores dropped 50%
featured.take: Students leaned on AI, then scored 50% lower without it. That's the gap between AI that does the work for you and AI that makes you better at the work. Run the same test on your business: if unplugging the AI craters your results, you built dependence, not capability.

featured.headline: Lovable reportedly in talks to double its valuation to $13.2B
featured.take: Money is pouring into tools that let anyone build an app in an afternoon. Good — but a huge valuation says nothing about whether the AI feature you ship with one actually works. Build fast if you like; just measure the result before you tell a customer it's good.
